headline: Asia-Pacific markets tumble as investors brace for a prolonged war in Middle East
summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in a late Thursday speech that the Strait of Hormuz, a vital artery for global oil trade, should remain shut.

headline: Exxon, Chevron, and other US oil and gas producers and refiners hit all-time-high stock values amid Iran war while consumers pay the price
summary: The U.S. aims to fill gaps in global oil and gas demand from the Iran war, but big worldwide shortfalls mean big profits.

headline: US to Ease Shipping Rule in Bid to Tame Spiraling Fuel Prices
summary: “In the interest of national defense, the White House is considering waiving the Jones Act for a limited period of time to ensure vital energy products and agricultural necessities are flowing freely to US ports,” White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said in a statement.  The plan comes as President Donald Trump considers multiple options to stem the dramatic rise in crude and gasoline amid the war in Iran.

headline: Why The Valero Energy (VLO) Narrative Is Shifting On Refining Outlook And Analyst Revisions
summary: Valero Energy’s fair value estimate has shifted from US$198.22 to US$202.72 per share, putting a finer point on what analysts think the stock might be worth based on refreshed assumptions. That move sits against a backdrop of mixed Street commentary, with some firms lifting targets and others trimming them or pulling back on previously strong endorsements, all tied to different views on refining resilience and crude markets. As you read on, you will see how these moving pieces fit together...

headline: Forget Nvidia And Micron — The Iran War Just Created An Earnings Boom For US Refiners
